description_long: This research institute is a large, rectangular building with a flat roof and two prominent, slightly raised sections featuring pyramidal glass skylights. The exterior is finished in a light beige or off-white material, likely concrete or stone, with minimal ornamentation and a strong emphasis on geometric forms. The facade includes deep-set windows and recessed entryways, with a symmetrical arrangement and a sense of solidity and permanence. The roof sections are punctuated by glass panels, allowing natural light into the central interior spaces. The building sits on a paved base with adjacent landscaped areas, and there are small exterior features such as signage and a possible information board near the entrance. The overall design is functional and institutional, with a focus on durability and clarity of form.
description: A large, light-colored research institute with flat roofs, geometric forms, and glass skylights, featuring deep-set windows and minimal exterior ornamentation.
description_view_0: Side view showing two main building volumes with glass skylights and a paved area with landscaping on the left.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the symmetrical facade, main entrance, and a row of windows along the lower section.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with both raised roof sections and skylights visible, as well as a paved area on the right.
description_view_3: Rear view showing a solid wall with deep-set windows and the central skylight, plus a small sign or board near the entrance.
attribute_signage: A rectangular sign is visible near the main entrance, mounted on the wall, with dark text on a light background. The text is not fully legible but appears to indicate the building's function or name.
attribute_entryway_design: The main entryway is centrally located on the front facade, recessed into the building with a rectangular shape. It features a wide, flat approach with steps leading up to double doors, likely made of glass or metal, and is flanked by deep-set windows. There are no visible accessibility ramps or decorative elements beyond the functional signage.
